item.title: Tinder Is Betting Gen Z Daters Would Rather Be Offline
item.description: The dating app’s turnaround bets on live events, group dating and an AI-heavy redesign.

item.title: France’s Moulin Says Deficit Situation Serious, Not Catastrophic
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France said the country has a serious challenge to reduce its budget deficit, but the situation isn’t catastrophic.

item.title: Iran War Oil Disruption Prompts Nigeria Producers to Lift Output
item.description: Nigerian oil companies are plowing windfall gains from the Iran-war crude rally into near-term extraction projects, boosting the drive by Africa’s top producer to double output within four years.

item.title: ECB’s Wunsch Sees June Hike Quite Likely If War Doesn’t End
item.description: There’s a high probability that the European Central Bank will raise borrowing costs next month if the Iran war doesn’t end in the meantime, according to Governing Council member Pierre Wunsch.

item.title: Iceland Delivers Second Hike in Two Months to Cool Economy
item.description: Iceland’s central bank raised borrowing costs for a second time since the outbreak of the Middle East war to quell persistent price pressure.

item.title: China’s Palladium Imports Surge as Local Futures Lure Inflows
item.description: China imported a record amount of palladium last month, as local prices that have raced ahead of global ones lured inflows.

item.title: Billionaire Family’s Firm Taps Rothschild for Rare Qatar IPO
item.description: A Qatari conglomerate that’s part of the billionaire Al-Khayyat family’s empire is working with Rothschild & Co. on a potential initial public offering of its healthcare unit, according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be a rare listing on one of the Middle East’s quieter exchanges.

item.title: France’s Moulin Says ECB Must Be Ready to Act With Eye on Growth
item.description: Emmanuel Macron’s nominee to run the Bank of France said the European Central Bank must be ready to tighten monetary policy as inflation pressure rises, though he stressed that officials should also consider how their actions affect economic expansion.

item.title: Veteran UBS Analyst Says AI Is Helping End China Property Slump
item.description: UBS Group AG real estate analyst John Lam, a market veteran who spotted problems at China Evergrande Group before his peers, has joined a chorus of observers declaring that China’s yearslong property downturn is finally losing steam.

item.title: South African Inflation Hits 20-Month High on Iran War Shock
item.description: South African annual inflation accelerated to the highest level in 20 months in April, driven by sharp increases in domestic fuel costs stemming from the Iran war.

item.title: Cyprus Gets Back EU Finance Ministers Scared Off by Drone Attack
item.description: For Cyprus’s embattled tourist industry, this week’s arrival of 27 European Union finance ministers with an entourage of central bankers and officials is better late than never.

item.title: Japan’s Nikkon Said to Weigh Going Private, US Funds May Bid
item.description: Nikkon Holdings Co. is considering going private and the Japanese logistics company is preparing to hold a first round of bidding in early June, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Drugmakers, Car Sales Help Extend Danish Economic Expansion
item.description: Denmark’s economy grew for a fourth straight quarter mainly on the strength of its pharmaceuticals industry, highlighting the Nordic nation’s relative resilience as it braces for the fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Japan Trade Minister to Attend APEC in China Amid Strained Ties
item.description: Japan’s Trade Minister Ryosei Akazawa will attend a gathering of trade chiefs hosted by China this week as Tokyo seeks to keep dialogue open with Beijing amid tense relations.

item.title: Gilts Jump as UK Inflation Surprise Curbs Rate-Hike Wagers
item.description: Gilts rallied as a sharper-than-expected slowdown in UK inflation prompted traders to trim bets on a Bank of England interest-rate hike next month.

item.title: Europe Delivers Best Earnings in Three Years But Hurdles Loom
item.description: Europe’s first-quarter earnings season was the best one in three years, buoyed by energy majors and tech giants. The performance may be difficult to sustain as the economic outlook sours and the war persists.

item.title: Bank Indonesia Surprises With a Half-Point Hike to Defend Rupiah
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank delivered a larger-than-expected interest-rate increase to intensify its defense of the rupiah after the currency tumbled to successive record lows this month.

item.title: GHO and CBC Combine to Form $21 Billion Healthcare Asset Manager
item.description: Global Healthcare Opportunities and CBC Group have agreed to combine to create a healthcare investment firm with more than $21 billion of assets under management, according to a statement Wednesday.

item.title: S. Korea’s Lee Urges ‘Limit’ to Labor Action Amid Samsung Unrest
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung called for an “appropriate limit” on collective labor action, speaking at a cabinet meeting after government-mediated talks between Samsung Electronics Co. and its union broke down, paving the way for a strike.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Aims to Avoid Large Bond Sales in Funding Plan
item.description: Japan’s Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said she expects to compile an extra budget without relying too heavily on bond issuance for the funding.

item.title: Citi Sees India Tightening Currency Controls to Halt Rupee Slump
item.description: India may take a number of steps in coming months to bolster foreign reserves and the rupee, including possibly restricting outflows from businesses, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: M&S Expects Profit Boost as Retailer Moves on From Cyber Hit
item.description: Marks & Spencer Group Plc expects annual profit to exceed the level seen before the British fashion and food chain was knocked off course by a cyberattack last year.

item.title: South Africa Plans Steel-Duty Hikes to Stem Industry ‘Emergency’
item.description: South African trade authorities proposed wide-ranging increases in tariffs on steel imports in an attempt to provide safeguards as an “unprecedented emergency” batters the nation’s industry.

item.title: UK Inflation Drops Sharply in Respite from Iran Energy Shock
item.description: UK inflation fell to its lowest rate in more than a year, prompting traders to bet on fewer Bank of England interest-rate hikes even though economists expect price pressures to return.

item.title: Africa’s Biggest Fund Manager in Talks to Buy Stake in Balwin
item.description: South Africa’s Public Investment Corp. is in talks to buy out minority shareholders of real estate developer Balwin Properties Ltd.

item.title: UK Bond Yields Fall on Inflation Slowdown
Global Funds Ready for Further Rupee Weakness With 100 in Sight
item.description: Global investors expect the Indian rupee to weaken further, with some even gaming out scenarios where the currency falls to an unprecedented 100 per dollar.

item.title: UAE’s Averi Targets Johannesburg Listing Through Reverse Merger
item.description: Dubai-based Averi Finance is in talks with Mantengu Ltd. over a reverse takeover of the South African mining firm that would result in the Middle Eastern company listing in Johannesburg.

item.title: Indian Central Bank Intervenes as Rupee Drops to Record Low
item.description: India’s central bank intervened to shore up the struggling rupee as elevated oil prices pushed the currency to a record low.

item.title: Global Long Bond Yields Climb to Highest in Almost Two Decades
item.description: The selloff in longer-maturity government bonds has pushed up yields to levels last seen during the global financial crisis, and strategists are warning the losses have room to run.
